title: Predict the Output: Matrices and Lists

questions.question: What is the output of this code?
questions.explanation: The matrix is filled column-wise: first column: 1,2; second column: 3,4. So row1, col2 is 3.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: [[2]] extracts the second element of the list, which is the character vector "hello".

questions.question: Predict the result:
questions.explanation: df[2, "y"] selects the second row of column 'y', which is "b".

questions.question: What does `length()` return?
questions.explanation: The list has two top-level components: first is a vector of length 3, second is a nested list. So length is 2.

questions.question: How many columns are in the resulting data frame?
questions.codeSnippet:
v1 <- c(TRUE, FALSE)
v2 <- c(10, 20)
df <- data.frame(v1, v2)
ncol(df)
questions.explanation: data.frame combines the two vectors as two columns, so ncol returns 2.
